strategy_candidates.title: Step-by-Step Writing Flow
strategy_candidates.short_description: A chronological breakdown of how a user actually interacts with the DiaryMate interface.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: The passage introduces a 'procedure.' Breaking this into discrete steps (Step 1: Title/Content, Step 2: Requesting Help, Step 3: Choosing Mode) directly addresses the 'flow' the user is struggling to visualize.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Using the text and the nearby context, describe the DiaryMate writing process as a numbered sequence of user actions and AI responses. Start from the initial entry and move through the assistance request.

strategy_candidates.title: Guided vs. Unguided in Action
strategy_candidates.short_description: A side-by-side comparison using a hypothetical diary entry scenario.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: The user is stuck on the conceptual difference between the two modes. A concrete example (e.g., writing about a 'busy day') shows exactly how 'keywords' (guided) differ from 'sentence completion' (unguided).
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Create a brief scenario where a user is writing a diary entry. Show what the AI would suggest if the user chose 'Guided Authoring' (using keywords) versus 'Unguided Authoring' (based on existing text).

strategy_candidates.title: System Input-Output Map
strategy_candidates.short_description: A map of what the user provides and what the AI generates for each mode.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: This reduces information density by focusing only on the data exchange, helping the user understand the 'mechanics' of the collaboration.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Define the 'Input' (what the user gives) and the 'Output' (what the AI provides) for both Guided and Unguided authoring modes based on the passage context.

strategy_candidates.title: The Core Difference
strategy_candidates.short_description: Focuses exclusively on the single most important distinction in the text.
strategy_candidates.why_recommended: When confusion is rising, narrowing the focus to one specific detail—how the AI knows what to write—can provide a 'foothold' for understanding the rest.
strategy_candidates.prompt_instruction: Explain the single biggest difference between the two modes: the use of 'keywords' versus 'existing content' as the trigger for AI generation.

trigger_reason: confusion confidence remained above threshold for 8 seconds while the user had an active selection
